log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable tagged as a 'spreader' with an overlay, which is a common indicator of malware designed to propagate. The size is 264KB. I have already seen network activity showing DN

The IOC summary confirms 2 unique IPs, 3 domains, 1 dropped file, and 1 process created. No registry persistence keys were found. I need to investigate the network activity in detail to understand the
The network activity confirms the sample queries 'ipv4bot.whatismyipaddress.com' to resolve an IP (66.171.248.178) and then makes an HTTP GET request to 'http://politiaromana.bit/'. This is a classic
The dropped file 'brfjdf.exe' is the payload that the initial sample (likely a dropper) downloaded and executed. The path shows it was written to the user's Application Data folder, which is a common
The process activity shows only 'nslookup politiaromana.bit ns1.virmach.ru' was created, which is the initial dropper. The dropped file 'brfjdf.exe' was written to disk but may not have been executed
The registry changes show no persistence keys or other registry artifacts. This suggests the malware might be a one-time dropper that downloads and executes a payload but doesn't establish persistent
The sandbox summary confirms the activity: 12 files opened, 1 file written (brfjdf.exe), 1 process created (nslookup), and 32 modules loaded. The presence of a PCAP and the fact that only the initial
